steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice C
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice C (C(d)=56d-28):
For $d=1$, expected cost is \$56.
$$C(1)=56\cdot1-28=28$$
Since $28\neq56$, choice C fails.

steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice A
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice A (C(d)=28d+28):
For $d=1$, expected cost is \$56.
$$C(1)=28\cdot1+28=56$$
For $d=2$, expected cost is \$56+\$28=\$84.
$$C(2)=28\cdot2+28=84$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
